question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R6 shuts down GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (the R6↔R7 link). The lower-core
path R2-R6-R7-R4 breaks. The upper-core path R2-R3-R4 absorbs all
traffic between PE-A and PE-B. Loss of path redundancy — if upper-core
fails next, no alternate route. ECMP between upper and lower core
collapses to single-path on every router. Excellent test of the
agent's ability to detect topology/redundancy degradation.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.106 (R6), GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (the R6<->R7 link) was administratively shut down.
Disabling the interface tears down the lower-core path R2-R6-R7-R4, so all PE-A<->PE-B traffic collapses onto the single remaining upper-core path R2-R3-R4 and ECMP across the two cores is lost. Reachability is preserved, but path redundancy is degraded - a second upper-core failure would isolate the PEs.
Fix: no shutdown on R6's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1.
expected_symptoms_summary:
Interface state change (%PKT_INFRA-LINK-5-CHANGED):
- R6: GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 → Administratively Down.

revert.commands:
configure terminal
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1
 no shutdown

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:01:14.032 UTC: ifmgr[333]: %PKT_INFRA-LINK-5-CHANGED : Interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1, changed state to Administratively Down
